title: Built two Polymarket trading bots — obsessed over making paper trading actually realistic. Live dashboards up for a week
author: FossilBlade
selftext:
Been working on a couple of automated trading bots for Polymarket's BTC 5-minute binary markets. Before going live with real money, I wanted paper trading results I could actually trust — not the usual "assume instant fills at mid price" nonsense that makes every backtest look like a money printer.

**The Bots:**

* **Grind Bot** — Plays the BTC 5-min Up/Down markets with a progressive sizing strategy. Manages risk across a streak of bets, adjusting position size based on recent outcomes to recover drawdowns and lock in gains.
* **Pulse Bot** — Takes positions on both sides of the BTC 5-min markets simultaneously, dynamically adjusting allocation based on real-time BTC price signals, candle patterns, and momentum indicators.

**What went into making the paper engine realistic:**

* **VWAP fills against real order books** — orders walk the actual live book depth, not some fantasy flat price
* **Queue position tracking** — limit orders sit in a simulated queue based on real resting depth at placement time. Queue drains as real trades execute at that level. No cutting the line.
* **Latency simulation** — orders aren't eligible for fills instantly, there's a simulated network delay window
* **Adverse price shift** — taker orders account for the price moving against you while your order travels
* **Real maker/taker fee structure** — maker orders pay zero fees, taker sweeps pay the actual Polymarket fee rate
* **Depth consumption** — fills actually eat through book levels, partial fills happen when liquidity is thin
* **Chainlink on-chain resolution** — no relying on Polymarket's UI to tell me who won

Dashboards are live for the next week:

* **Grind Bot:** [https://polybots.51qcd.pro/grind/](https://polybots.51qcd.pro/grind/)
* **Pulse Bot:** [https://polybots.51qcd.pro/pulse/](https://polybots.51qcd.pro/pulse/)

If the numbers hold up after a week of real-market paper trading, I'll switch to live and keep the dashboards public so you can track performance.

And yes, Claude was my co-pilot on this one. I'm not going to pretend otherwise. At the end of the day, it's the numbers on the dashboard that matter — the bot either makes money or it doesn't, regardless of how the code got written.

comments.author: GirlFromUpNorth95
comments.body: Are you also going to run these in a shadow-trading style as your next step?

replies.author: FossilBlade
replies.body:
Yup, live and paper both running via the same bot. Thats the only way to truly compare what's the drift in paper and live. I want to make paper engine as close as possible to real so I am reliably dev/test other bot.

That's why created 2 bots that run at different pace. 

Grind is very slow bases on only one order, pulse is frequent with many different types of  orders
